posts.text:
I just found this posting from Hostinger for a paid hosted version of my project Rackula on a VPS. 

Rackula is under the MIT license, so commercial use is OK. Though I note they even use the logo, though they don't link the actual repo.

I partially take this as a win: this is the first time I've ever seen something I made been sold as a service. 

It also makes me think I need to be more cautious with my work/license.

I'm curious, any other dev perspectives or similar experiences to this?

Rackula v26.6.4 was released... yesterday! I meant to mention it. I've done a huge UX overhaul, with more to come. I'm learning to use my crayons properly and not eat them.

https://github.com/RackulaLives/Rackula/releases/tag/v26.6.4

Claude Opus 4.8 in Ultracode mode is a beast: ran a 24 agent adversarial review of the Rackula codebase, finding 7 new issues and 3 existing. Now batching the fixes. 

It was even smart enough to know it's own container has an 8GB heap, so sizing to not hit OOM. 

This was started from my laptop, while I'm sitting in my car at a ferry terminal. 

I find that super cool 🤓

Listening to Uhh Yeah Dude ep 1099 - Seth is going off about how AI will ruin sports over the next 10 years because kids, rather than just trying real hard like Larry Bird, they’ll just go to Claude and build a training plan.  Coach Claude is gonna be our downfall. 

As someone who regularly uses Claude for pretty much every challenge in my life, I  absolutely agree that this sort of exporting our self direction to a computer is probably going to have significant consequences.

I've been trying to make the most of my Claude subscription, fully aware of the ever-present looming thread of Anthropic and other frontier model providers halting their subsidized approach to subscriptions. 

According to CodexBar, I've used the equivalent of $6,246.08 USD of tokens in the last 30 days at API prices. That is roughly 32x the price I am paying right now.
